title: Sole Proprietor DBA Agreement
description: Agreement establishing sole proprietor business guidelines, DBA registrations, and liability separation statements.
whatsCovered: DBA registration declarations and county locations
Ownership of trade names, logos, and digital domains
Separation of business banking and personal accounting files
Acknowledgment of unlimited personal liability of owner
whenToUse: Use this contract when operating as an unregistered sole proprietor under a Doing Business As (DBA) trade name.

clauses.heading: SOLE PROPRIETOR DBA AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Sole Proprietor DBA Agreement. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. Trade Name Usage
clauses.body: Owner operates as a sole proprietor using Doing Business As (DBA) trade name: {dba_trade_name}. Owner has sole rights to trade names.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
